key_insight: A DSL-guided LLM agent can improve inverse materials design by grounding natural-language rules in symbolic predicates and statistical evidence.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical path to faster, more interpretable discovery of candidate perovskite oxides without requiring large task-specific training datasets.
method: Translate natural-language design rules into chemistry DSL predicates.
Use accumulated materials data to test, validate, and refine rules during iterative agentic search.
Deploy a multi-agent framework (ORCHESTRA) for inverse design of double perovskite oxides under multiple target-property objectives.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that DSL-guided symbolic predicates helped the agent identify unsupported rules, validate new rules, and improve the rule store across iterative design cycles, with better performance than natural-language-only rules.
watch_item: Performance gains depend on the quality and coverage of the DSL, data availability for statistical grounding, and careful handling of unsupported or incorrect rules.
paper_title: Symbolic Predicate-Guided Language Agents for Inverse Design of Perovskite Oxides
